questions.text: What is 25% expressed as a fraction?
questions.explanation: 25% means 25 out of 100, which simplifies to \(\frac{1}{4}\).

questions.text: Convert \(\frac{3}{5}\) to a percentage.
Express 0.45 as a percentage.
What is 15% of 200?
If 40 is 20% of a number, what is the number?
questions.explanation: Let number be x, \(20\% \times x = 40 \Rightarrow x = 200\).

questions.text: Convert 75% to decimal form.
What percent is \(\frac{7}{10}\)?
Find 10% of 350.
What is 5% of 1,000?
Express 0.125 as a percentage.
If a shirt costs ₹500 and is sold at 10% discount, what is the discount amount?
A shopkeeper sells an item for ₹1,200 which cost him ₹1,000. What is the profit percent?
What is 50% of 2,000?
If 18 is 60% of a number, what is the number?
questions.explanation: Let number be x, \(60\% \times x = 18 \Rightarrow x = 30\).

questions.text: If a quantity increases from 400 to 460, what is the percentage increase?
A man bought a bicycle for ₹5,000 and sold it for ₹4,500. What is the loss percent?
What is 125% of 80?
questions.explanation: 125% of 80 = \(\frac{125}{100} \times 80 = 100\). (Correction: 125/100*80=100) Actually 125% of 80 = 1.25 × 80 = 100. So correct answer should be 100, option a.

questions.text: If 30% of a number is 90, what is 50% of the same number?
questions.explanation: Let number be x, \(30\% \times x = 90 \Rightarrow x = 300\). So 50% of x = 150.

questions.text: A shopkeeper offers 20% discount on a book priced at ₹250. What is the selling price?
If the price of an item decreases from ₹1,500 to ₹1,350, what is the percentage decrease?
A shopkeeper sold a shirt for ₹850 after giving a discount of 15% on the marked price. What was the marked price of the shirt?
questions.explanation: Let the marked price be ₹x. After 15% discount, selling price = 85% of x = ₹850. So, 0.85x = 850, x = 850 ÷ 0.85 = ₹1000.

questions.text: Ravi bought a bicycle for ₹12000 and sold it for ₹13200. What is the percentage profit he made?
A student scored 72 marks out of 90 in an exam. What percentage did he score?
The price of a packet of rice increased from ₹400 to ₹460. What is the percentage increase in price?
A shopkeeper offers a 20% discount on a toy. If the toy’s marked price is ₹500, what is the price after discount?
A jacket is sold for ₹1500 after a 25% discount. Find the marked price of the jacket.
questions.explanation: Selling price = 75% of marked price = ₹1500. Marked price = 1500 ÷ 0.75 = ₹2000.

questions.text: A shopkeeper bought a watch for ₹800 and sold it at a 12.5% profit. Find the selling price of the watch.
questions.explanation: Profit = 12.5% of 800 = ₹100. Selling price = 800 + 100 = ₹900. (Check options carefully; option a is ₹900, correct answer.) Correction: Profit = 12.5% of 800 = 0.125 × 800 = ₹100. Selling price = 800 + 100 = ₹900. So correct answer is a.

questions.text: A mobile phone was priced at ₹12000 but is now sold at ₹10800. What is the percentage decrease in the price?
A shopkeeper gives a discount of 10% on a book. If the discounted price is ₹450, what was the original price?
questions.explanation: Discounted price = 90% of original price = ₹450. Original price = 450 ÷ 0.9 = ₹500.

questions.text: A farmer sold 60% of his 500 kg wheat crop. How many kilograms of wheat did he sell?
A shopkeeper sold an article at a 20% profit. If the selling price is ₹720, what was the cost price of the article?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price be ₹x. Profit = 20% of x = 0.20x. Selling price = cost price + profit = x + 0.20x = 1.20x. Given selling price = ₹720, so 1.20x = 720 ⇒ x = 720 / 1.20 = ₹600.

questions.text: A jacket originally priced at ₹1500 is sold after a discount of 15%. If the shopkeeper wants to make a profit of 10% on the cost price, what is the cost price of the jacket?
questions.explanation: Selling price after 15% discount = 1500 - 15% of 1500 = 1500 - 225 = ₹1275. Let cost price be ₹x. Profit = 10% of x = 0.10x. Selling price = cost price + profit = x + 0.10x = 1.10x. Given selling price = ₹1275, so 1.10x = 1275 ⇒ x = 1275 / 1.10 = ₹1159.09 approximately. But options closest to this is ₹1300 (correct calculation shows option c is closest). Rechecking: 1.10x=1275 ⇒ x=1275/1.10=1159.09, so ₹1150 is closer. So correct answer is b.

questions.text: A mobile phone's price increased by 12% in January and then decreased by 10% in February. If the original price was ₹10,000, what is the price after February?
questions.explanation: Price after January increase = 10,000 + 12% of 10,000 = 10,000 + 1,200 = ₹11,200. Price after February decrease = 11,200 - 10% of 11,200 = 11,200 - 1,120 = ₹10,080.

questions.text: A student scored 72 marks which is 80% of the total marks. If the total marks are increased by 25%, what will be the new total marks and what percent does 72 marks represent of the new total?
questions.explanation: Original total marks = 72 ÷ 0.80 = 90. New total marks after 25% increase = 90 + 25% of 90 = 90 + 22.5 = 112.5. Percentage of 72 marks in new total = (72 / 112.5) × 100 = 64%.

questions.text: A trader bought goods worth ₹18,000 and sold them at a 15% profit. Later, he offered a discount of 10% on the selling price. What is the final price at which the goods were sold?
questions.explanation: Selling price before discount = 18,000 + 15% of 18,000 = 18,000 + 2,700 = ₹20,700. Discount = 10% of 20,700 = 2,070. Final price = 20,700 - 2,070 = ₹18,630. Since ₹18,630 is not in options, rechecking calculations: 15% profit on 18,000 = 2,700; selling price = 20,700; 10% discount = 2,070; final price = 20,700 - 2,070 = 18,630. Closest option is ₹17,820 (option b). Possibly a mistake in options; correct final price is ₹18,630.

questions.text: A shopkeeper increases the price of an article by 25% and then offers a discount of 20% on the increased price. What is the net percentage increase or decrease in the price?
questions.explanation: Let original price = ₹100. After 25% increase, price = 100 + 25 = ₹125. After 20% discount, price = 125 - 20% of 125 = 125 - 25 = ₹100. So, net change = 100 - 100 = 0, which means no net increase or decrease. Correct answer is a.

questions.text: Convert \( \frac{7}{20} \) into a percentage.
questions.explanation: To convert \( \frac{7}{20} \) into percentage, multiply by 100: \( \frac{7}{20} \times 100 = 35% \).

questions.text: Express 0.48 as a percentage.
questions.explanation: Multiply the decimal by 100 to get percentage: \(0.48 \times 100 = 48%\).

questions.text: Find 15% of 240 rupees.
What percent of 500 is 125?
A shirt originally priced at ₹800 is sold at a discount of 12.5%. Find the discount amount and the selling price.
accepted_forms: 100 rupees discount and 700 rupees selling price
Discount is 100 and selling price is 700
questions.explanation: Discount = 12.5% of 800 = \( \frac{12.5}{100} \times 800 = 100 \) rupees. Selling price = 800 - 100 = 700 rupees.

questions.text: The price of a book increases from ₹120 to ₹138. Find the percentage increase.
A shopkeeper buys an article for ₹450 and sells it for ₹495. Find the profit percent.
Convert 62.5% into a fraction in simplest form.
If 40% of a number is 160, find the number.
questions.explanation: Let the number be x. Then 40% of x = 160, so \( \frac{40}{100} \times x =160 \) => \( x = \frac{160 \times 100}{40} = 400 \).

questions.text: A jacket is sold for ₹1,200 after a 20% discount. Find the original price of the jacket.
questions.explanation: Let original price be x. Selling price = 80% of x = 1200, so \( \frac{80}{100} \times x = 1200 \) => \( x = \frac{1200 \times 100}{80} = 1500 \) rupees.

questions.case_text: Ramesh bought a bicycle for ₹8,000. After a year, he sold it for ₹9,200. He then used the money to buy a new bicycle which was offered at a 10% discount on the marked price. The marked price of the new bicycle was ₹12,000.

subparts.text: What was the percentage profit Ramesh made when he sold his old bicycle?
subparts.explanation: Profit = Selling Price - Cost Price = ₹9,200 - ₹8,000 = ₹1,200. Percentage profit = (Profit / Cost Price) × 100 = (1200 / 8000) × 100 = 15%.

subparts.text: What was the price Ramesh paid for the new bicycle after the discount?
How much more money did Ramesh need to pay to buy the new bicycle after selling the old one?
subparts.explanation: Money from selling old bicycle = ₹9,200. Price of new bicycle = ₹10,800. Additional money needed = ₹10,800 - ₹9,200 = ₹1,600.

questions.case_text: A shopkeeper bought 500 packets of rice at ₹40 each. He sold 300 packets at a 5% profit and the remaining 200 packets at a 10% loss. He wants to find his overall percentage profit or loss.

subparts.text: What was the selling price of one packet sold at 5% profit?
subparts.explanation: Selling price at 5% profit = Cost Price + 5% of Cost Price = ₹40 + (5/100 × 40) = ₹42.

subparts.text: What was the selling price of one packet sold at 10% loss?
subparts.explanation: Selling price at 10% loss = Cost Price - 10% of Cost Price = ₹40 - (10/100 × 40) = ₹36.

subparts.text: What was the overall percentage profit or loss on the entire transaction?
subparts.explanation: Total cost price = 500 × ₹40 = ₹20,000. Total selling price = (300 × ₹42) + (200 × ₹36) = ₹12,600 + ₹7,200 = ₹19,800. Overall loss = ₹20,000 - ₹19,800 = ₹200 loss. Percentage loss = (200 / 20,000) × 100 = 1%. So, there is 1% loss, not 0%. Correction: The correct answer is 1% loss.
